stem: A researcher collected data on daily high temperature ($x$, in degrees Fahrenheit) and the number of ice cream cones sold per day ($y$) at a beachside stand. The line of best fit for the data is $y = 1.6x - 48$. On a day when the high temperature was $85°$F, the stand actually sold 98 ice cream cones. What is the residual for that day?
explanation:
The residual is defined as: residual $=$ actual value $-$ predicted value.

Step 1: Use the line of best fit to find the predicted number of ice cream cones sold when $x = 85$.
$$y = 1.6(85) - 48 = 136 - 48 = 88 \text{ cones}$$

Step 2: Calculate the residual.
$$\text{residual} = \text{actual} - \text{predicted} = 98 - 88 = 10$$

The residual is $10$, indicating the stand sold 10 more cones than the line of best fit predicted for that temperature.

distractorRationale.A: This results from computing only the slope term when finding the predicted value, then subtracting from the actual: $98 - 1.6(85) = 98 - 136 = -38$. The student applied the slope but forgot to subtract the constant $-48$ from the equation (i.e., did not complete the prediction step).
distractorRationale.B: Sign error. This results from reversing the residual formula, computing predicted minus actual instead of actual minus predicted: $88 - 98 = -10$.
distractorRationale.C: Correct. The predicted value is $1.6(85) - 48 = 88$, and the residual is $98 - 88 = 10$.
distractorRationale.D: Incomplete solution. This is the predicted number of cones from the line of best fit ($y = 88$). The student correctly computed the prediction but stopped before calculating the residual.
